commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: feat(feeds): add read-only feed discovery
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `feeds` extension for curated skill/plugin catalog sources and read-only discovery.

This PR establishes the primitive without install enforcement:

- Adds `plugins.entries.feeds.config.sources[]` for configured feed documents.
- Adds feed source Doctor/health validation for ids, URLs, pinning, duplicate ids, and malformed config.
- Adds `openclaw feeds sources`, `feeds list`, and `feeds search`.
- Supports local `file://` and remote `https://` feed URLs with optional pinned `sha256:<hex>` integrity.
- Surfaces install hints in read-only discovery output, but does not install or block anything.
- Adds plugin docs and plugin inventory wiring.

## Example syntax

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "feeds": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "sources": [
            {
              "id": "company-approved",
              "url": "https://feeds.example.com/openclaw/feed.json",
              "trust": "pinned",
              "integrity": "sha256:..."
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Commands

```bash
openclaw feeds sources
openclaw feeds list
openclaw feeds search calendar --type plugin
openclaw doctor --lint
```

## Notes

This is intentionally read-only. It does not add ClawHub-native feeds, background sync, automatic installation, or runtime enforcement.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The bundled feeds plugin can be enabled from config and the real OpenClaw CLI can read configured feed sources, list entries, and search feed entries after the branch was rebased and the remote HTTPS fetch path was moved behind the SSRF guard.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 as root, source checkout /root/src/openclaw-feeds-87824 at 504426827e699f0309e6c27e8a53eecd92ebf9b2, Node.js v24.15.0, repo source runner scripts/run-node.mjs, isolated OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json and OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state. The proof feed used a local file:// source so no external service credentials were required.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Created /tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json with plugins.entries.feeds.enabled=true and one source: { id: "local-proof", url: "file:///tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/feed.json", trust: "unsigned" }.
2. Ran OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds sources --json.
3. Ran OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds list --json.
4. Ran OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds search proof --type plugin --json.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "sources": [
    {
      "id": "local-proof",
      "url": "file:///tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/feed.json",
      "enabled": true,
      "trust": "unsigned"
    }
  ]
}
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "type": "skill",
      "id": "proof-skill",
      "name": "Proof Skill",
      "description": "Local proof skill feed entry",
      "tags": ["proof", "feeds"],
      "sourceId": "local-proof",
      "feedId": "proof-feed"
    },
    {
      "type": "plugin",
      "id": "proof-plugin",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "name": "Proof Plugin",
      "description": "Local proof plugin feed entry",
      "tags": ["proof", "plugins"],
      "sourceId": "local-proof",
      "feedId": "proof-feed"
    }
  ]
}
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "type": "plugin",
      "id": "proof-plugin",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "name": "Proof Plugin",
      "description": "Local proof plugin feed entry",
      "tags": ["proof", "plugins"],
      "sourceId": "local-proof",
      "feedId": "proof-feed"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
All three real CLI commands exited 0. The CLI loaded the enabled feeds plugin from the isolated config, reported the configured source, listed both feed entries, and filtered search results to the matching plugin entry.

What was not tested:
No external HTTPS feed endpoint was contacted in the real CLI proof. The HTTPS path is covered by the focused CLI test added in this branch, which asserts default HTTPS loads call fetchWithSsrFGuard with auditContext "feeds.feed-document" and report SSRF guard blocks to the operator.
## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

Focused stack proof on the final condensed branch: 243 tests passed.

## Feed PR stack

1. openclaw/openclaw#87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. openclaw/openclaw#87825 - approved feed installs
3. openclaw/openclaw#87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. openclaw/openclaw#87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. openclaw/openclaw#88732 - native feed search defaults and policy checks
6. openclaw/clawhub#2460 - ClawHub root feed lanes

The stack keeps OpenClaw as a feed consumer. ClawHub root feeds are producer infrastructure; enterprise or tenant feeds can be produced elsewhere using the same schema.

RFC draft: https://github.com/giodl73-repo/rfcs/blob/feeds-rfc-draft/rfcs/0004-feeds.md
